---

## Purpose

The Project Management Plan (PMP) is the master document that defines how [[PROJECT_NAME]] will be executed, monitored, controlled, and closed. It integrates all subsidiary management plans and baselines, providing a comprehensive roadmap for project delivery. This plan serves as the primary reference for the project team and stakeholders, ensuring consistent approach to project management throughout the project lifecycle.

---

## Scope

This Project Management Plan covers:
- Project lifecycle and methodology
- Integration of all subsidiary plans
- Baseline management (scope, schedule, cost)
- Change control processes
- Configuration management
- Project reviews and governance
- Closure criteria and procedures

This plan references but does not duplicate:
- Detailed scope definitions (Scope Management Plan)
- Detailed schedules (Schedule Management Plan)
- Detailed budgets (Cost Management Plan)
- Risk details (Risk Management Plan)

---

## Objectives

1. **Integration:** Unify all project management activities into a cohesive approach
2. **Guidance:** Provide clear direction for project execution
3. **Baseline:** Establish approved baselines for scope, schedule, and cost
4. **Control:** Define processes for monitoring and controlling project work
5. **Communication:** Ensure all stakeholders understand project management approach
6. **Consistency:** Apply standardized practices across the project

---

## Instructions for Completion

1. **Project Overview:** Define project context, objectives, and success criteria
2. **Methodology Selection:** Choose and document project lifecycle approach
3. **Baseline Establishment:** Create and approve scope, schedule, and cost baselines
4. **Subsidiary Plans:** Develop and integrate all subsidiary management plans
5. **Change Control:** Define change management processes and authority levels
6. **Review Schedule:** Establish regular review and update cadence
7. **Approval:** Obtain formal approval from Executive Sponsor and key stakeholders
8. **Communication:** Distribute to all project participants and ensure understanding

# 2. PROJECT LIFECYCLE AND METHODOLOGY

## 2.1 Development Approach

**Selected Methodology:** [[AGILE / WATERFALL / HYBRID]]

### Methodology Justification
[[RATIONALE_FOR_SELECTED_APPROACH]]

### Hybrid Approach Details (if applicable)
| Phase | Methodology | Rationale |
|-------|-------------|-----------|
| Discovery | [[METHOD]] | [[RATIONALE]] |
| Design | [[METHOD]] | [[RATIONALE]] |
| Development | [[METHOD]] | [[RATIONALE]] |
| Testing | [[METHOD]] | [[RATIONALE]] |
| Deployment | [[METHOD]] | [[RATIONALE]] |

## 2.2 Project Phases

### Phase 1: Discovery & Requirements
**Duration:** [[DURATION]]  
**Deliverables:**
- Stakeholder analysis
- Requirements specification (PRD)
- Technical approach document
- Project charter (approved)

**Exit Criteria:**
- [ ] Signed PRD
- [ ] Approved technical approach
- [ ] Resource commitment confirmed
- [ ] Risk assessment completed

### Phase 2: Design & Architecture
**Duration:** [[DURATION]]  
**Deliverables:**
- System architecture
- UI/UX designs
- Database schema
- API specifications

**Exit Criteria:**
- [ ] Design approval from Product Owner
- [ ] Technical review completed
- [ ] Security review passed

### Phase 3: Development
**Duration:** [[DURATION]]  
**Deliverables:**
- Developed features
- Unit tests
- Technical documentation
- Code repository

**Exit Criteria:**
- [ ] 80% feature completion
- [ ] Code review completed
- [ ] Unit test coverage >[[PERCENTAGE]]%

### Phase 4: Quality Assurance
**Duration:** [[DURATION]]  
**Deliverables:**
- Test plans and cases
- Test execution reports
- Defect reports
- UAT completion

**Exit Criteria:**
- [ ] All P0/P1 defects resolved
- [ ] Security audit passed
- [ ] Performance benchmarks met
- [ ] UAT sign-off

### Phase 5: Deployment & Handoff
**Duration:** [[DURATION]]  
**Deliverables:**
- Production deployment
- Documentation package
- Training completion
- Acceptance certificate

**Exit Criteria:**
- [ ] Production deployment successful
- [ ] Smoke tests passed
- [ ] Documentation delivered
- [ ] Training completed
- [ ] Signed acceptance

# 4. BASELINE MANAGEMENT

### Scope Baseline
**Components:**
- Project Scope Statement
- Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
- WBS Dictionary

**Baseline Date:** [[DATE]]  
**Current Version:** [[VERSION]]

**Critical Path:** [[ACTIVITIES]]

**Components:**
- Cost Estimates by WBS Element
- Time-Phased Budget (S-Curve)

**Baseline Date:** [[DATE]]  
**Current Version:** [[VERSION]]

## 4.2 Baseline Change Control

### Change Thresholds

| Baseline Element | Minor Change | Major Change | Critical Change |
|------------------|--------------|--------------|-----------------|
| **Scope** | < [[PERCENTAGE]]% effort | [[PERCENTAGE]]-[[PERCENTAGE]]% | > [[PERCENTAGE]]% |
| **Schedule** | < [[DAYS]] days | [[DAYS]]-[[DAYS]] days | > [[DAYS]] days |
| **Cost** | < [[PERCENTAGE]]% | [[PERCENTAGE]]-[[PERCENTAGE]]% | > [[PERCENTAGE]]% |

### Change Approval Authority

| Change Type | Approver | Timeline |
|-------------|----------|----------|
| Minor | Product Owner | 24 hours |
| Major | Steering Committee | 1 week |
| Critical | Executive Sponsor | 48 hours |

---

# 5. CHANGE CONTROL PROCESS

## 5.1 Change Control Board (CCB)

### CCB Composition
| Role | Name | Responsibility |
|------|------|----------------|
| Chair | [[PRODUCT_OWNER]] | Final approval authority |
| Member | [[PROJECT_MANAGER]] | Impact assessment |
| Member | [[TECHNICAL_LEAD]] | Technical feasibility |
| Member | [[QA_LEAD]] | Quality impact |
| Member | [[FINANCE_REP]] | Budget impact (if applicable) |

### CCB Operating Procedures
- **Meetings:** [[WEEKLY/BI-WEEKLY/AS_NEEDED]]
- **Quorum:** [[NUMBER]] members required
- **Decision Method:** [[CONSENSUS/MAJORITY_VOTE]]
- **Documentation:** All decisions recorded in Change Log

## 5.2 Change Request Process

### Step 1: Request Submission
- Change Request form completed
- Initial impact assessment provided
- Submitted to Project Manager

### Step 2: Impact Analysis
- Scope impact assessment
- Schedule impact analysis
- Cost impact calculation
- Risk impact evaluation
- Quality impact review

### Step 3: CCB Review
- Presentation of change and impacts
- Discussion and questions
- Decision: Approve / Reject / Defer / Request More Info

### Step 4: Decision Communication
- Decision communicated to requestor
- If approved: Update baselines and plans
- If rejected: Document rationale
- If deferred: Schedule for future review

### Step 5: Implementation (if approved)
- Update affected plans and baselines
- Communicate to team
- Track implementation
- Validate completion

## 5.3 Emergency Change Process

**Trigger:** Critical issue requiring immediate action

1. **Assessment:** Project Manager and Technical Lead assess urgency
2. **Authorization:** Product Owner provides verbal/email approval
3. **Implementation:** Execute change immediately
4. **Documentation:** Complete Change Request form retroactively within 24 hours
5. **Validation:** CCB reviews at next meeting for ratification

## 5.4 Change Log

| CR ID | Date | Description | Requestor | Impact | Decision | Approved By | Status |
|-------|------|-------------|-----------|--------|----------|-------------|--------|
| CR001 | [[DATE]] | [[DESC]] | [[NAME]] | [[IMPACT]] | [[DECISION]] | [[NAME]] | [[STATUS]] |

## 6.3 Configuration Control Process

1. **Identification:** New configuration item identified
2. **Control:** Check-in/checkout procedures
3. **Status Accounting:** Track versions and changes
4. **Audit:** Periodic verification of configuration items
5. **Release Management:** Controlled release of configurations

---

# 7. PROJECT REVIEWS

## 7.1 Review Schedule

| Review Type | Frequency | Participants | Purpose |
|-------------|-----------|--------------|---------|
| **Daily Standup** | Daily | Core team | Coordination, blockers |
| **Sprint Review** | Bi-weekly | Team + PO | Demo, feedback |
| **Phase Gate Review** | Per phase | All stakeholders | Phase completion |
| **Monthly Status Review** | Monthly | Steering Committee | Executive oversight |
| **Quality Review** | Monthly | QA, TL, PM | Quality metrics |
| **Risk Review** | Bi-weekly | PM, Team | Risk status |

## 7.2 Phase Gate Review Process

### Pre-Review (1 week before)
- [ ] Deliverables completed and submitted
- [ ] Phase documentation updated
- [ ] Quality checks passed
- [ ] Review agenda distributed

### Review Meeting
1. **Opening:** Review objectives and agenda (5 min)
2. **Deliverable Review:** Walk through phase outputs (30 min)
3. **Criteria Validation:** Verify exit criteria met (15 min)
4. **Risk Assessment:** Review updated risks (10 min)
5. **Decision:** Go / No-Go / Go with Conditions (10 min)
6. **Next Phase Planning:** Preview upcoming work (10 min)

### Post-Review
- [ ] Decision documented
- [ ] Action items assigned
- [ ] Baselines updated (if approved)
- [ ] Communication distributed

---

# 8. PROJECT CLOSURE

## 8.1 Closure Criteria

Project closure will be initiated when ALL of the following are satisfied:

- [ ] All deliverables completed and accepted
- [ ] Acceptance Certificate signed by Executive Sponsor
- [ ] Final payment processed (if applicable)
- [ ] Knowledge transfer completed
- [ ] Documentation delivered
- [ ] Warranties and support agreements in place
- [ ] Lessons learned documented
- [ ] Project archive completed

## 8.2 Closure Activities

| Activity | Owner | Timeline | Deliverable |
|----------|-------|----------|-------------|
| Final acceptance | PO | Week 1 | Signed certificate |
| Financial reconciliation | PM | Week 1 | Final budget report |
| Documentation handover | TL | Week 1-2 | Complete doc package |
| Knowledge transfer | Team | Week 1-2 | Training records |
| Contract closure | PM | Week 2 | Closed contracts |
| Lessons learned | PM | Week 2 | Lessons learned doc |
| Archive | PM | Week 2 | Project archive |
| Celebration | PM | Week 2 | Team recognition |

## 8.3 Early Termination Criteria

Project may be terminated early if:
- Business case is no longer valid
- Funding is withdrawn
- Strategic priorities change
- Unresolvable technical barriers
- Force majeure events

**Termination Process:**
1. Executive Sponsor initiates termination review
2. Impact assessment completed
3. Termination plan developed
4. Stakeholder notification
5. Closeout activities executed
6. Final report submitted

---

# 9. GLOBAL DEFINITION OF DONE

## 9.1 Feature-Level Definition of Done

A feature is considered "Done" when:

- [ ] Code implemented according to requirements
- [ ] Unit tests written and passing (>[[PERCENTAGE]]% coverage)
- [ ] Code reviewed and approved
- [ ] Integration tests passing
- [ ] Documentation updated (technical and user)
- [ ] No critical or high defects open
- [ ] Product Owner acceptance
- [ ] Merged to main branch
- [ ] Deployed to staging environment

## 9.2 Sprint-Level Definition of Done

A sprint is considered "Done" when:

- [ ] All committed features meet feature-level DoD
- [ ] Sprint demo completed
- [ ] Stakeholder feedback incorporated
- [ ] Sprint retrospective completed
- [ ] Sprint metrics captured
- [ ] Next sprint planned

## 9.3 Release-Level Definition of Done

A release is considered "Done" when:

- [ ] All planned features complete
- [ ] System testing passed
- [ ] Performance testing passed
- [ ] Security testing passed
- [ ] UAT completed and signed
- [ ] Documentation complete
- [ ] Deployment plan approved
- [ ] Rollback plan tested
- [ ] Go-live authorized
